summary: Verified the ESLint react-hooks/exhaustive-deps fix in Experience.jsx (bucket variable used in useEffect cleanup instead of timers.current). CI=true production build passes with no warnings/errors (98.88 kB gzipped). Smoke-tested Experience section on the preview URL — timeline renders 3 entries, clicking updates the detail panel correctly, 'Experience loaded.' terminal line shows, and the blinking amber cursor animates. Regression checks (About Me, Case Studies with Bracket + Open Bracket CTA + media, Behind The Pixels, Peek '/' overlay, Cmd+K palette) all intact.
critical_code_review_comments: Experience.jsx useEffect cleanup pattern is now correct — const bucket = timers.current; is captured at effect-run time, satisfying react-hooks/exhaustive-deps. No behavior change.
CI=true yarn build reports 0 warnings/errors — Vercel deploy should now succeed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 9 verified only the ESLint build-fix in Experience.jsx and did a smoke test of the Experience section + regression on About Me, Case Studies, Behind The Pixels, Peek and Cmd+K palette. Deeper testing already covered in iterations 1-8. Preview URL: https://saksham-design-1.preview.emergentagent.com. Contact form is MOCKED (localStorage only).
rca of the issue: Vercel CI=true treats ESLint warnings as errors. The react-hooks/exhaustive-deps warning fired because the useEffect cleanup closed over timers.current directly — ref values can change by the time cleanup runs. Fix: capture const bucket = timers.current; inside the effect body and reference bucket in the cleanup (idiomatic React pattern). Confirmed with local CI=true yarn build success.
